meta.toolResultPreview: 1: # GSD Hook Architecture - Canonical Source & Install Pipeline 2: 3: > **TL;DR:** The hook files in `.agent/hooks/`, `.claude/hooks/`, `.gemini/hooks/`, and 4: > `.opencode/hooks/` are **not copies** - they are **OS hardlinks** sharing a single inode. 5: > Editing any one of them instantly edits all of them. There is no maintenance hazard. 6: > This document explains why, how the pipeline works, and what the rules are for contributors. 7: 8: --- 9: 10: ## Table of Contents 11: 12: 1. meta.preview: Let's start with the JS module architecture doc.

vision.rationale: The vision is expressed primarily through workflow files, especially new-project.md which describes GSD as 'the most leveraged moment in any project - deep questioning here means better plans, better execution, better outcomes.' The COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md together articulate the cross-harness unified workflow vision. No dedicated VISION.md or README.md exists at root level.

architecture.rationale: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md is an explicit, detailed architecture document covering the hardlink model, 3-stage install pipeline, harness coverage, runtime self-detection, and hook inventory. The .gsd/bin/ tree and settings.json files also document the runtime architecture.

principles_guidelines.rationale: COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md contains an explicit 'Rule for Future Maintenance' section and documents the cross-harness prefix convention. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md Section 6 is titled 'Rules for Contributors'. These together constitute the principles and guidelines for the project.
